302	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
303	
304	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
305	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
306	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
307	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
308	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
309	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
310	Picard presheaf at `T`.
311	
312	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
313	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
314	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
315	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
316	
317	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
318	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
319	
320	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
321	
322	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
323	presheaf
324	```
325	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
326	```
327	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
328	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
329	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
330	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
331	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
332	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
333	
334	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
335	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
336	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
337	
338	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
339	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
340	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
341	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
342	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
343	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
344	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
345	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
346	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
347	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
348	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
349	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
350	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
351	  r L L' := Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)
352	  iseqv :=
353	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
354	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
355	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
356	
357	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
358	
359	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
360	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
361	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
362	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
363	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
364	on quotients
365	```
366	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
367	```
368	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
369	
370	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
371	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
372	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
373	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
374	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
375	
376	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
377	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
378	
379	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
380	
381	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
382	is a canonical map
383	```
384	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
385	```
386	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
387	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
388	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
389	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
390	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
391	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
392	
393	iter-186: with the iso-class setoid `preimage_subgroup`, `Quotient.lift`
394	on `L ↦ ⟦(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L⟧` is well-defined because
395	functors preserve isomorphisms. The identity / composition laws giving the
396	full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging follow from `pullbackId` /
397	`pullbackComp` and are iter-187+ work (they currently aren't needed by any
398	downstream consumer). -/
399	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
400	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
401	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
402	  let g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
403	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
404	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
405	  Quotient.lift
406	    (fun L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT =>
407	      Quotient.mk (preimage_subgroup πC πT')
408	        (⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L.carrier,
409	          L.isLocallyTrivial.pullback g_C⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT'))
410	    (fun _ _ ⟨e⟩ => Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).mapIso e⟩)
411

234	under tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of
235	`[L]` is `[L⁻¹] = [Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)]`). The pullback map
236	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_k T)` of

240	products (Stacks 01HH for invertibility, Mathlib's
241	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` for the underlying multiplicativity).

244	quotient `Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` inherits a canonical
245	abelian-group structure under which the quotient map is a surjective

  open-assignment `y ↦ V y` and the per-point chart-`IsIso` hypothesis just built, concluding
  `IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N)`. Package as `asIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N)` for the `Iso`
  conclusion; naturality in `M, N` (if consumed downstream) is `pullbackTensorMap_natural`
  (`lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural`), already unconditional and not part of this statement's
  type. `f^*M, f^*N, f^*(M⊗N)` being locally trivial on this same cover follows from
  `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` and `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`
  (`lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback`, `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`) — consumed
  implicitly by the chart-chase shape, not as a separate obligation of this statement. -/
noncomputable def pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    (M N : X.Modules) (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ≅
      tensorObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N) := by
  -- For each `y : Y`, build a chart of the common trivialising cover and feed `chart_isIso`.
  have key : ∀ y : Y, ∃ V : Y.Opens, y ∈ V ∧
      IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor V.ι).map (pullbackTensorMap f M N)) := by
    intro y
    -- Common trivialising affine `W ∋ f.base y` on `X`, refining charts of `M` and `N`.
    obtain ⟨UM, hxUM, _, ⟨eM0⟩⟩ := hM (f.base y)
    obtain ⟨UN, hxUN, _, ⟨eN0⟩⟩ := hN (f.base y)
    obtain ⟨W, _, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
      exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := f.base y) (U := UM ⊓ UN) ⟨hxUM, hxUN⟩
    have hWUM : W ≤ UM := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left

to the identity functor. -/

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
